g.data-narration: Welcome to learning about Continuous Internal Evaluation - your pathway to mastering Operating Systems concepts.
CIE is a formative assessment system that evaluates your learning throughout the semester through multiple components.
g.text: Continuous Internal Evaluation is a systematic assessment framework
mandated by university to evaluate student performance throughout the semester.
• Formative assessment • Progressive learning • Multiple components

g.data-narration: CIE helps bridge theory with practice in OS topics like virtual memory, page replacement, and file systems.
g.text: CIE bridges theoretical knowledge (demand paging) with practical
• Identifies weaknesses early • Allows timely remediation • 40% of total marks

g.data-narration: CIE comprises three main components: Internal Assessment Tests, Assignments and Quizzes, and Lab Assessments.
IATs are two written exams covering 50 percent syllabus each - IAT-1 covers Modules 1-3, IAT-2 covers Modules 4-5.
g.text: Two written exams per semester, each covering 50% of syllabus:

g.data-narration: Assignments include theoretical comparisons like FIFO versus LRU, numerical EAT calculations, and programming tasks like simulating copy-on-write.
g.text: Compare FIFO vs LRU page replacement
Case study: NTFS vs EXT4 structures
EAT = (1-p) × Memory Access Time + p × Page Fault Overhead

g.data-narration: Lab assessments test practical skills: directory structures with tree algorithms, analyzing Belady Anomaly in FIFO, and fork system calls.
CIE marks are calculated by averaging IAT scores, adding assignment and quiz marks, with maximum CIE of 50 scaled to 40 percent.
g.text: CIE = (IAT-1 + IAT-2)/2 + Assignment Marks + Quiz Marks

g.data-narration: Let us calculate: IAT-1 is 45, IAT-2 is 38, Assignments are 14, Quizzes are 9. Average IAT is 41.5, total 64.5, scaled to 34.4 marks.
In OS exams, expect questions like calculating page faults using Optimal and LRU with given reference strings and frame counts.
g.text: Given page reference string: 7,0,1,2,0,3,0,4,2,3,0,3,2,1,2,0,1,7,0,1 with 3 frames

g.data-narration: OS concepts in CIE prepare you for real tasks: debugging race conditions, designing file access control, and memory optimization in embedded systems.
g.text: Kernel Dev • Sys Admin • Cloud Infrastructure

g.data-narration: For exams: master page replacement algorithms, memorize inode structures, allocate study time by weightage, and practice previous IAT questions.
Avoid common mistakes: do not ignore non-IAT components, practice algorithms with reference strings, and prepare for scenario-based questions.
g.text: Quizzes on COW carry 15% weight - do not neglect them
Must calculate page faults for given reference strings

g.data-narration: Key takeaways: CIE contributes 50 percent to final grade, IATs test applied knowledge, and CIE emphasizes concept integration across OS modules.
Master OS through CIE: practice page fault calculations, solve previous IATs, use active recall, and prepare for scenario-based questions.
g.text: Your Path to Success in OS
Practice Page Fault Calculations • Solve Previous IATs • Use Active Recall
Prepare for Scenario-Based Questions • Manage Time Effectively
